pages.title: About Rachel - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South and South Bedfordshire - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South & South Bedfordshire
pages.text_excerpt: About Rachel - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South and South Bedfordshire - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South & South Bedfordshire Skip to main content In Luton South and South Bedfordshire Constituency News In Parliament Parliamentary News Parliamentary Contributions Written Questions Newsletter Sign up for my email updates! About Rachel Contact Rachel Office Standards More Menu About Rachel Having served as the MP for Luton South between 2019 and 2024, it’s a privilege to be elected to represent the new constituency of Luton South & South Bedfordshire in Parliament. Bedfordshire is my county, and Luton is my home. I was born in the Luton & Dunstable hospital and have lived in Luton almost all my life. I’m proud of our area and all our communities, whether they be urban or rural. Throughout my life I have always been guided by a commitment to public service. Before entering Parliament, I was a senior manager in the public sector and a local councillor for nine years. I will always be a voice for all of our communities from the House of Commons green benches. After 14 years of the Conservatives, people are worse off, and nothing works anymore. Our Labour Government is turning the page on decline and despair, prioritising wealth creation to make the country better off, restoring economic stability and cutting NHS waiting lists. Together, we can deliver our mission of national renewal and change. Contact details Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Rachel Hopkins both at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.text_excerpt: Contact Rachel - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South and South Bedfordshire - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South & South Bedfordshire Skip to main content In Luton South and South Bedfordshire Constituency News In Parliament Parliamentary News Parliamentary Contributions Written Questions Newsletter Sign up for my email updates! About Rachel Contact Rachel Office Standards More Menu Contact Rachel Follow me on social media Get in Touch: For assistance, advice or policy queries, you can get in touch via: Email: [email protected] Phone: 01582 457 774 Post: Rachel Hopkins MP, 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN You can also complete the online form below. Please include your full name, address and contact details in any correspondence. How Can I Help? I can offer assistance with a wide range of issues including immigration, housing allocations and repairs, employment, Universal Credit, child maintenance and other local government issues. Please remember to include your full name, postal address, email and telephone number. When contacting me in respect of a personal casework matter, please also include any relevant reference numbers (such as your Home Office reference number or National Insurance number). Such details will help my staff to deal with your case more efficiently. It is important to note that Members of Parliament are prohibited from giving legal advice. MPs also do not have any powers to interfere or intervene with decisions made by local councils or government departments such as the DWP or the Home Office. For legal representation, please contact the Luton Citizens Advice Bureau who can refer you for legal help. Contact Rachel Your Name * Email Address * Address * Postcode * Contact Telephone Number Please provide an outline of your issue, including any reference numbers Name This field is for validation purposes and should be left unchanged. Advice Surgeries I offer monthly face-to-face advice surgeries. To book an appointment, please email [email protected] and please remember to include your name, address and telephone number, along with a brief outline of the issue as we might be able to offer immediate help. Urgent Assistance Should you be in need of urgent assistance (especially when our office is closed), the following organisations may be able to help you: Samaritans –call: 116 123, text: 07725 90 90 90 or email [email protected] Childline – counselling and advice for under 19s. 0800 1111 www.childline.org.uk National Domestic Violence Helpline – 0808 2000 247 www.refuge.org.uk Women’s Aid Refuge – Office hours – 01582 391856 https://womensaidinluton.org/ Police – To make a non-urgent crime report phone 101 or report online https://www.beds.police.uk/ Age UK – 0800 678 1602 https://www.ageuk.org.uk/bedfordshire/ StreetLink – 0300 500 0914 https://www.streetlink.org.uk/ Citizens Advice Bureau – 0800 144 8848 https://www.citizensadviceluton.org.uk/ Luton Council – office hours 01582 546000 https://www.luton.gov.uk/ Central Bedfordshire Council – 0300 300 8301 https://www.centralbedfordshire.gov.uk/ Privacy Policy You can read my full privacy policy statement here. Contact details Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Rachel Hopkins both at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: New clean energy action to help ease cost of living - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South and South Bedfordshire - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South & South Bedfordshire
pages.h1: New clean energy action to help ease cost of living
pages.text_excerpt: New clean energy action to help ease cost of living - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South and South Bedfordshire - Rachel Hopkins MP for Luton South & South Bedfordshire Skip to main content In Luton South and South Bedfordshire Constituency News In Parliament Parliamentary News Parliamentary Contributions Written Questions Newsletter Sign up for my email updates! About Rachel Contact Rachel Office Standards More Menu 22nd April, 2026 New clean energy action to help ease cost of living Rachel Hopkins MP in Luton I’m very pleased to back new measures announced this week to ramp up the government’s clean energy mission and protect families in Luton South and South Bedfordshire from shocks on the global stage. Energy Secretary Ed Miliband has announced a number of new policies to go further in delivering clean, homegrown energy we can control. These plans will ensure long term energy security for Britain and help cut the cost of living for working people – safeguarding them from the consequences of conflicts abroad, such as the war in Iran. New measures will include making it easier for renters, those who live in flats, or those without a drive to install EV chargers, solar panels and heat pumps, giving more people the opportunity to take advantage of cheaper, cleaner energy. The plans will also see a massive expansion of solar panels and wind turbines across the public estate, including on brownfield and industrial sites or railways, as well as a huge overhaul of planning to speed up connecting new projects to the grid and upgrading existing renewables. Alongside this, the Chancellor has announced plans to break the link between gas and electricity prices, which unfairly drives up costs even though a significant share of Britain’s electricity now comes from renewables and nuclear. To shield families from the impact of future crises, the government is expanding long-term fixed contracts and increasing the Electricity Generator Levy. This will ensure that an increased proportion of the money generated when gas prices spike is available to government to support businesses and households. This action on clean energy comes alongside a number of new measures which came into force this month to support families, including an average saving of £117 on energy bills, record rises in both the National Minimum and Living Wage, a £575 boost for millions of pensioners, and the removal of the two-child benefit cap- helping to raise the living standards of over £5,000 children in Luton South and South Bedfordshire. This Labour Government is on your side, stepping in when times are tough. Whether that is immediate solutions like taking money off energy bills and freezing rail fares, or making the long-term decisions that will bring costs down for good, we are determined to put more money back in your pocket. I am proud to support these new policies that will drive our clean energy mission forward and protect family budgets for years to come. Contact details Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Rachel Hopkins both at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
